pageContext.name: Distracted Boyfriend (Set of 2)
pageContext.description: I should dump him for good this time.   Sideshow Art Prints presents the Distracted Boyfriend Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) by artist ukiyomemes, blending Japanese art history with modern meme culture to create a high-end and iconic addition to any art collection.  Ukiyo-e, or pictures of the floating world, is a Japanese art movement from the 17th century that emphasizes beauty and pleasure in life through a variety of subjects from history and folk tales. The style became popular with merchants prosperous enough to indulge in the privilege of decorating their homes with artwork — and now you can do the same, with a modern twist!  Artist ukiyomemes blends the classic art sensibilities with the most popular visual language of the current moment, internet memes. The Distracted Boyfriend Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) turns a pair of viral stock photos into a dramatic tale of disloyalty in the Edo period. On the left canvas, a woman wearing a red kimono fans herself while a caption above reads, A beauty is strolling among the shops unaware that she is being watched by a fool with bad intentions. On the right canvas, a man in a blue kimono remarks to himself, Hahaha, what a hottie. I always say, 'Just because you are on a diet doesn't mean you can't look at the menu.' Beside him, a woman in a blue kimono looks on in disgust, thinking, I can't believe it! He's up to his bad behavior again! I should dump him for good this time.   The Distracted Boyfriend Canvas Art Print Set is officially licensed by Memes of the Floating World, AKA ukiyomemes, and each individual image comes printed on Aurora Natural Canvas, adding depth and texture to the composition. The gallery wrapped canvas prints also feature sawtooth hanging hardware installed for ease of display, and archival inks ensure the longevity of this amusing scene in your collection.  Elevate your humor to the floating world and bring home the Distracted Boyfriend Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) today!
